authoritative_sources.title: Federal Acquisition Regulation Part 15
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary legal baseline for proposal solicitation, competition, and source-selection workflow in federal procurement contexts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Proposal intake, compliance sequencing, and submission readiness

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS Part 215
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defense-specific source-selection and proposal-management baseline that supersedes generic acquisition assumptions in this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Defense-specific proposal operations and handoff validation

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.215-7009, Proposal Adequacy Checklist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defense-specific checklist provision for adequate cost/pricing proposals, used as an authoritative evidence-completeness boundary when the solicitation invokes it.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Proposal adequacy and completion evidence review
used_for: completion refusal when required proposal artifacts are absent

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS Subpart 204.73, Safeguarding Covered Defense Information and Cyber Incident Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative control for handling covered defense information; directly affects whether proposal and readiness artifacts may move across systems and owners.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Special Publication 800-171 Revision 3
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official control framework for safeguarding CUI and related audit evidence used in defense proposals and readiness packets.
used_for: escalation when security context is unknown

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA production-conformity framework provides authoritative boundaries for release control and production evidence expectations relevant to hold/release posture in aerospace contexts.

authoritative_sources.title: IAQG 9100 Quality Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized aerospace quality standard defining record and process quality expectations, including traceability and supplier interfaces.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Traceability, supplier control, and release-readiness handling

authoritative_sources.title: DoD Manual 4140.01 Volume 11, Inventory Accountability and Special Management and Handling
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public DoD supply-chain manual describing inventory accountability and special handling for controlled and critical items, making it authoritative for proposal readiness claims tied to inventory and supplier evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Inventory accountability, special handling, and supplier-evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.02, Operation of the Defense Acquisition System
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines defense lifecycle governance, helping distinguish proposal operations handoff boundaries from downstream engineering and authority shifts.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS Sector Reference
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official aerospace-and-defense sector taxonomy provides the baseline terminology and classification context for defense manufacturing proposal records.
used_for: boundary prevention against generic proposal behavior

workflow_stages.name: Proposal intake and authority capture
workflow_stages.description: Validate request scope, mission criticality, and required authority ownership before any execution update.

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and traceability assembly
workflow_stages.description: Gather production lot/serial lineage, ERP/MES/WMS traces, supplier certifications, quality-hold state, and security handling tags before progress transitions.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Cross-system progression and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Assess dependencies across ERP, MES, supplier portals, and readiness systems; route missing or conflicting cases to exception queue and adjacent owners.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Release-boundary and completion confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Issue completion evidence only when holds are addressed, approver routes are complete, and release conditions are not conflicting.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and adaptation management
workflow_stages.description: When source-policy conflicts, mission-readiness ambiguity, or safety constraints appear, escalate and preserve evidence rather than completing.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: Production orders and execution work requests
Batch, lot, or serial genealogy records
Inventory availability and WMS allocation records
Quality holds, nonconformance reports, and release notices
Change-control records, safety/mission readiness notices, and maintenance deferral logs

allowed: Progress execution state when required evidence is complete and owners and release gates are clear.
Summarize traceability conflicts and inventory evidence state.
Prepare completion records, exception queues, and readiness summaries for downstream operators.
escalate: Required tenant policy or approval maps are missing and materially change the disposition.
Lot, supplier, or release records materially conflict across systems.
Submission readiness, safety, or mission constraints change after drafting progress guidance.
External legal, financial, safety, or supplier-approval authority is required.
refuse: Finalize completion or release when evidence gates remain unresolved.
Authorise substitution or hold bypass without approved supplier-release authority.
Move CUI or sensitive evidence without required control metadata.

domain_failure_modes: Marking completion without full lot/serial traceability and release evidence.
Collapsing quality hold or nonconformance boundaries into operational completion.
Accepting supplier substitutions or inventory substitutions without authorized owner.
Allowing one system of record to dominate without resolving ERP/MES/WMS conflict.
Escalating legal, contract, safety, or financing decisions as proposal operations actions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Execution exception requiring rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific mission readiness thresholds and safety-stop definitions
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Tenant policy may tighten readiness and maintenance-deferral thresholds; these are treated as required runtime inputs and do not block package operation without retrieval fallback.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Supplier substitution approval patterns across classes of supply
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Flowdown-specific supplier substitution limits vary by facility and customer. Lane escalates such requests unless tenant-approved authority is present.

notes: Research sources are restricted to authoritative public acquisition, aerospace, and security references and are mapped to workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling.
No in-package claim is made for legal, engineering, or release authority; all such cases escalate through adjacent specialist routing.
